task_completion.description: Open the Graph Coloring activity in GCompris
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window is open and displaying the 'Graph Coloring' activity.

task_completion.description: Play the first level of the activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level indicator in the GCompris UI shows that the first level (e.g., level 1, or 1 star) is currently active.

task_completion.description: Color all nodes in the graph
task_completion.visual_evidence: Every node in the displayed graph has been filled with a color; no nodes are left empty or in their default uncolored state.

task_completion.description: Ensure no two connected nodes share the same color
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine every line (edge) connecting two nodes; the nodes at opposite ends of any given line must have different colors.
A success indicator is visible on the screen, such as a smiling Tux penguin, a 'Good'/'Congratulations' message, or a prompt to proceed to the next level.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the solved state to '~/graph_solved.png'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a screenshot command (e.g., scrot, maim, or gnome-screenshot) targeting '~/graph_solved.png' or '/home/ga/graph_solved.png', or a file manager/image viewer confirming the file exists at that path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GCompris user interface, including its menu bar and graphical elements, is visible in the screenshots, rather than a mock-up or image editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to solve the puzzle
integrity.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is seen interacting with the nodes to change their colors, rather than the puzzle being solved instantly via terminal commands or config file manipulation.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is authentic and matches the agent's gameplay
integrity.visual_evidence: The image saved to ~/graph_solved.png visually matches the final solved state achieved by the agent in the GCompris window, not a stock image or downloaded file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The activity is 'Graph Coloring' in GCompris. The goal is to color graph nodes so that no two adjacent nodes share the same color. The expected screenshot path is exactly '/home/ga/graph_solved.png'.
